fix: correct RSA padding, improve login UX, add README

- Fix PKCS#1 v1.5 padding: use 0x00 0x01 prefix (was missing leading
  zero byte, causing signature verification failures)
- Switch login to clipboard-based URL reading (pbpaste) to handle
  long Amazon redirect URLs that exceed terminal paste buffers
- Add HTTP client timeout (30s) to prevent indefinite hangs
- Default author to "Unknown" when --author not provided
- Update module path to github.com/juange87/kindlecli
- Add comprehensive .gitignore for credentials and IDE files
- Add detailed README with usage, architecture, and project structure
- Add implementation plan document

commit.message:
Fix dark mode and language not persisting across refreshes

Dark mode: replaced manual darkMode state + localStorage with
next-themes useTheme() hook, which was already set up in layout.tsx
but being bypassed. This eliminates the flash and double-click issue.

Language: initialize state from localStorage synchronously instead of
in useEffect, preventing the flash from English to saved language.

commit.message:
docs: add skill.md references to all installation guides

Every tool's setup now includes instructions to add the skill.md
reference to project rules or system prompts. This teaches AI tools
the behavioral guidelines for using pıut's MCP tools — when to call
get_context, reading soul first, using update_brain vs add_memory, etc.

Tool-specific guidance:
- Coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, Zed):
  project rules files with skill.md URL reference
- Chat tools (ChatGPT, Msty): custom instructions/system prompt
- SDKs (OpenAI Agents, Claude Agent): fetch skill.md into system prompt
- Desktop apps (Claude Desktop): upload as Project Knowledge
- Gemini: note about MCP not supported yet, re-export needed

Also added "Why skill.md matters" section at the top explaining
that MCP provides the connection but skill.md provides the behavioral
guidance.

Rename headline from App Factory to App Engine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(security): detect CVE-2025-8747 get_file gadget bypass (#602)

* feat(security): detect CVE-2025-8747 Keras get_file gadget safe_mode bypass

Bypass of CVE-2025-1550 fix. Uses keras.utils.get_file as a gadget to
download and execute arbitrary files even with safe_mode=True. Detected
when config.json references get_file AND contains URL strings.

- Add _check_get_file_gadget() to keras_zip_scanner
- Flag get_file + URL combo as CRITICAL
- Add explanation function for CVE-2025-8747
- 4 new tests (detection, no URL no trigger, no false positive, attribution)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: consolidate duplicate [Unreleased] CHANGELOG section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(keras): require contextual get_file+URL evidence for CVE-2025-8747

* fix: resolve mypy errors and merge duplicate CHANGELOG sections

- Fix nodes variable redefinition in _iter_dict_nodes(): declare nodes
  once before branches with type annotation, use plain assignment inside
- Add tmp_path: Path annotation to _make_keras_zip() in test file and
  import Path from pathlib
- Move [Unreleased] section before [0.2.26] in CHANGELOG and merge
  both Added and Fixed subsections under a single header

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: trigger CI run

* fix(keras): harden CVE-2025-8747 get_file gadget attribution

* fix(security): restore CVE-2025-49655 detection removed by merge conflict

The merge from main incorrectly deleted all CVE-2025-49655 TorchModuleWrapper
detection code. This commit restores:
- _check_torch_module_wrapper and _is_vulnerable_keras_3_11_x methods
- get_cve_2025_49655_explanation function in explanations.py
- keras_version extraction from metadata.json
- TorchModuleWrapper detection in layer iteration loop
- CHANGELOG and conftest.py entries that were incorrectly removed
- Strengthened test assertions for cvss and remediation fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: trigger workflow

* fix: remove extra blank line in CHANGELOG for prettier formatting

commit.message:
feat: v2.9 — concurrent generation, login persistence, and 10 UI improvements

Sales tab:
- Concurrent Quick & Batch generation via background threads + polling
  (multiple generations run simultaneously; filters always interactive)
- Collapsible leads table (📊 expander, expanded by default)
- Full email subject shown in outreach history (removed [:55] truncation)
- Run Sales Agent button inline with "N leads matching filters"
- Right-panel toggle (🔍 Reasoning) isolates tool-call cards from main UI

Onboarding tab:
- Plans saved to Supabase + plan history section (like email history)
- PDF export of onboarding plan via fpdf2
- Promo override selectbox + ℹ description popover
- Timeline sorted chronologically by day (fixes Photography/QA ordering bug)
- Timeline milestones use coloured left-border cards (🟠/🔵/🟣) instead of dots
- Right-panel toggle for agent reasoning

Retention tab:
- Right-panel toggle for agent reasoning

Auth:
- Login persists across page refresh via streamlit-cookies-controller

Storage:
- Added load/save/clear_onboarding_plans for Supabase persistence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show clear error when new password matches current password

Detect Supabase 422 same_password error and show a specific message
instead of the generic fallback.

commit.message:
feat: Week 2 — per-page text extraction and OCR dispatch

- models.py: PageResult + PagesManifest Pydantic types for provenance
- pdf/text_extract.py: PyMuPDF native text extraction per page
- pdf/ocr.py: Tesseract OCR dispatch with mean confidence scoring
- pdf/ingest.py: full extraction pipeline replacing Week 1 placeholder
  - writes pages/page_NNNN.txt and pages/pages.json per run
  - method decision: pymupdf | tesseract | none based on ocr_mode + threshold
- config.py: add min_text_chars_for_native (default 50)
- pipeline/run.py: pass run_id to ingest; handle RuntimeError from failed open
- 15 new tests (25 total, all passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v2.4.0: addItems(), hide badge/arrow in single-column, docs update

- addItems(items): create and append <li> elements from an array of item
  objects (or JSON string); internally uses _createItemElement() per item
  then calls _initItems() once in a batch
- Badge now hidden in single-column mode (CSS display:none); arrow was
  already hidden — center-line elements have no meaning without a center line
- docs: addItems() + item schema documented, "Load via JSON" demo button
- README: addItems API, item schema, v2.4.0 changelog entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace farm sprites with new 4-frame construction sequence (#345)

## Summary
- Replace farm building art with new 4-frame isometric construction
sequence (tilled field → framework → barn → completed farm with crops)
- Update `farm.png` (finished sprite) and `farm_building_sequence.png`
(4-frame strip) with properly processed sprites: downscaled to 256×192
canvas, transparent background, content bottom-aligned to match
footprint
- Fix type inference parser error in `prototype_hud.gd` (`var count :=`
→ `var count: int =`)

## Test plan
- [ ] Place a farm in-game and verify 4 construction stages render
correctly
- [ ] Verify completed farm sprite aligns with footprint selection tiles
(no vertical offset)
- [ ] Verify transparent background (no white rectangle behind sprite)
- [ ] Verify player color shader still works on flag areas

fix: switch favicon service to DuckDuckGo to avoid 404 console errors

Google's faviconV2 returns 404 for sites without favicons, spamming the
console. DuckDuckGo's icon service returns a default icon instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: throttle NBA API retries after cold-start failure

Add try/except around NBA API calls in _get_team_stats() so
fail_ts is recorded on any exception. The existing cooldown
check then blocks thundering-herd retries for 60s and returns
stale data when available, preventing cascading 500s on Railway
cold start.

commit.message:
Fix: iOSで動画圧縮時に0Bファイルがアップロードされる問題を修正

- iOSではWebCodecs(mediabunny)の動画エンコードが不安定でファイル読み出しが
  途中で止まるため、iOS環境では動画圧縮をスキップするように変更
- mediabunnyが例外を投げた場合に元ファイルを明示的にセット
- アップロード直前に0Bチェックを追加し、元ファイルにフォールバック

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add ImmutableObjectRead operation to composite workload (#25646)

## Summary
- Add new `ImmutableObjectRead` benchmark operation to the composite
workload that exercises immutable object reads
- Add Move module `immutable_object.move` with `ImmutableData` struct
created via `transfer::freeze_object`
- Add `assert_reachable!` in `consensus_validator.rs` to verify
immutable claims verification code path is exercised

## Test plan
- [x] Ran `seed-search.py` with 10 seeds - all passed
- [x] Verified `assert_reachable!("transaction has immutable input
object claims")` is being hit (22/28 reachability assertions satisfied)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat: integrate Kotlin web module as native form engine in Job Tester

Replace the TypeScript form evaluation engine with the real Kotlin
WorkflowEngine compiled to JS, embedded as an iframe. This eliminates
fidelity bugs from the manual TS reimplementation.

- Modify Kotlin web module (Main.kt) for postMessage-driven iframe mode
- Add createStateWithContext() to PsSchemaStateBuilder for passing
  referenceData, savedWidgetData, formName, and isEnabled
- Create KotlinFormFrame.tsx iframe wrapper with postMessage bridge
- Replace FormRenderer with KotlinFormFrame in JobTesterCanvas
- Fix CalculationUtils.toPlainString stripping significant zeros on
  Kotlin/JS (2000 → "2" due to JS Double.toString() omitting ".0")
- Reset tester to setup phase when "Test with Job" is chosen from explorer
- Set active project context when testing a job from explorer
- Fix duplicate detection in portal scanner (match on name AND type)
- Fix bare field name resolution in formStateEngine
- Mark show-action fields as isHidden at parse time in schemaParser

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix icon and count badge vertical alignment with title first line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add claude stow package with headsdown focus session timer (#5)

- Add claude/.claude as a new stow package (settings.json, skills/, hooks/)
- Add /headsdown skill: starts a timed focus session by writing ~/.claude/temp/headsdown.json
- Add UserPromptSubmit hook that injects elapsed/target time into Claude's context each prompt
- Add SessionEnd hook that auto-clears the session file on exit
- Gitignore machine-local claude state (credentials, history, plugins, temp, memory, etc.)
- Register claude in config.ini stow_folders

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
